design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate platforming challenges, defeat enemies, progress through story-based levels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Combat and platforming obstacle avoidance as the Tick character
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as The Tick, the blue superhero from the animated series, using his strength and durability in action platforming scenarios
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Licensed IP platformer designed as a straightforward tie-in to The Tick animated series with story-structured progression. Emphasizes character authenticity and narrative progression over mechanical innovation.

difficulty.lives_system: Configurable lives (default supports up to 7 lives based on cheat menu)
difficulty.continues_system: Configurable continues (default supports up to 4 continues based on cheat menu)
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression with potential stage checkpoints typical of 1994 SNES platformers
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High - player can configure lives and continues via options menu, suggesting accessibility design. Level skip functionality (L+R) available via cheat code, indicating developer awareness of difficulty variance across player skill.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-structured level progression. Hidden level select accessible via cheat code (requires setting Lives=7, Continues=4, Arthurs=2 and playing Teleport in Test Sound menu)
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select enables non-linear replay
Cheat code system (unspecified codes present) suggests alternative playstyles
Level skip mechanic (L+R) enables speedrun or challenge-run variations

world_building_techniques: Level organization explicitly labeled by 'Story' in level select menu, indicating narrative structure
Character roster integration (Arthur character selectable in cheat menu, suggesting supporting cast involvement)
IP authenticity to animated series and comic source material

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - The Tick SNES page)
